url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation — Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §20 + §4 + §5 + §27.7 + §29 + §28 + §32 + §18 + §1 + §25 + §22 + §24 + §30.4 + §6 + §20.3 house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. RRA 2025 Sch 1 para 24 (Ground 8 13-week / 3-week notice / UC carve-out) verbatim-anchored at Stage 1 seed time per §16.27; TCGA 1992 s.106A 30-day matching rule + s.17/s.18 connected-persons + s.58 inter-spouse pre-locked at §24/§5/§1.O; TCPA 1990 s.55(1) + s.55(2)(f) verbatim-anchored; GPDO 2015 SI 2015/596 in-force + Class MA via SI 2021/428 anchored (Class MA verbatim conditions paginated past seed-time displayed segment — RUN re-verifies at write). §28 full Wave 8 cluster lock used as anchor for Part 8ZB / Part 9A picks (C7); §29 VAT architecture (reverse-charge s.7A + s.8; Sch 9 + Sch 10; TOMS s.53; Sch 1 threshold) used as anchor for OTA-host VAT (C11) + commercial service charge VAT (C13). RPDT IN-FORCE per FA 2022 ss.31-53 verbatim-anchored at §28.7 STATUS CORRECTED 2026-05-25 — propagated through C7 brief Do-not-write GREP to prevent drift to incorrect 'repealed by FA 2024 s.81' claim.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified RRA 2025 Sch 1 para 24 / HA 1988 s.8 + Sch 2 / ITTOIA 2005 ss.35 + 270 + 271A + 272A-B / TCGA 1992 ss.3 + 17 + 18 + 58 + 104 + 106A + 161 + 162 + 286 + s.38 / Sch 2 paras 6-12 / FA 2013 Part 3 (ATED) + Part 5 (GAAR) / FA 2003 s.116 / FA 2022 Part 2 (RPDT) + ss.31-53 / CTA 2010 Part 8ZB + Part 22 / ITA 2007 Part 9A / FA 2016 ss.77 + 79 + 81 + 82 / TCPA 1990 s.55 + s.106 / Use Classes Order SI 1987/764 + SI 2020/757 / GPDO 2015 SI 2015/596 + SI 2021/428 + SI 2024/579 / Planning Act 2008 Part 11 / CIL Regs SI 2010/948 / LURA 2023 Part 4 + s.81 / VATA 1994 s.7A + s.8 + Sch 9 Gr 1 + Sch 10 + Sch 7A Gr 6 + Sch 8 Gr 5 + s.53 + Sch 1 / SI 2023/817 / SI 2022/1265 / SI 2013/376 reg 58 / SI 2020/1311 / TCEA 2007 Part 3 / SI 2013/1894 / LTA 1985 ss.18-30 / LPA 1925 s.146 / CAA 2001 Part 2 + Part 2A / FA 2020 s.30 / TMA 1970 s.12B / FA 2025 Sch 5 / FA 2024 s.27 anchors at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §20 + §4 + §5 + §27.7 + §28 + §29 + §32 + §18 + §1 + §1.J + §25 + §22.1 + §24 + §30.4 + §6 + §20.3 + §1.O locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (continuing the pattern observed in M3-A-B1 + M3-A-B2 D-2 + M3-B-B1). Each extended brief landed at +157 to +181 lines of additional content (worked examples + 10-12 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 2 author entry). This pattern across A1/A2/B1/C1/C2 confirms that Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_F102_CANNIB_DISCIPLINE — pick 5 (C11 booking-com) carries F-102 cannib-watch against the C5 sibling airbnb-landlords. Stage 2 brief explicitly identifies five structural distinctions (reverse-charge VAT on Dutch B.V. commission vs Airbnb Payments UK Ltd; higher commission + Genius / Preferred Partner programmes; channel-manager + professional-host integration; no-show / strict-rate retention accounting; cross-OTA reconciliation). Anti-templating watchpoints + Do-not-write GREP list propagate F-102 vigilance to RUN session. The Booking.com-distinct framing IS the page; if it collapses into Airbnb-mirror at RUN, BRIEF_DRIFT escalation at gate.

D-4 STAGE2_CLUSTER_HUB_ORIENTATION — picks 2 (C7 investor-or-developer) and 3 (C8 short-guide-planning-permission) are both cluster orientation HUBS that forward-link extensively to deeper-mechanic pages rather than re-walking them. Stage 2 anti-templating notes explicitly identify the hub framing as the load-bearing differentiator vs Wave 8 Bucket B Part 8ZB / Part 9A statutory deep-dives (C7) and MW1 C1 CIL deep-dive + cluster-specific change-of-use pages (C8). Pick 6 (C13 commercial-service-charge) follows the same hub-orientation pattern relative to deeper VAT-option-to-tax and capital-vs-revenue clusters. RUN session must hold these three pages in the orientation lane.

D-5 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 + M3-A-B2 D-5 + M3-B-B1 patterns (~1.5-2 hours for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-6 STAGE2_SLUG_CLUSTER_MISMATCH_FLAGGED — pick 4 (C12 bed-and-breakfasting) is mis-clustered in the queue ('Tenancies and Renters' Rights Act' cluster label) but is plainly a CGT anti-avoidance concept routed to category `capital-gains-tax`. Stage 1 seed flagged this; Stage 2 brief carries the flag forward in both work logs. Manager Stage 1b decision pending on whether to formally re-cluster as 'CGT — disposal & specific scenarios' or 'Anti-avoidance and case law'. Not blocking the page (the CGT framing is the operative content); cluster label is administrative.

D-7 STAGE2_FA2026_S137_DRIFT_PREVENTED_BY_M3CB1 — the M3-C-B1 sibling brief (anti-avoidance-rules-share-exchanges-and-reorganisations) opened the F-101 §37 HP-lock candidate around the FA 2026 substitution of TCGA s.137(1) with new (1)-(1C) + (7) arrangements definition effective 18 March 2026. M3-C-B2 picks do not engage TCGA s.137 directly; however, the connected-persons + Ramsay-line discipline propagated through C12 (bed-and-breakfasting) brief shares conceptual territory with the FA 2026 amended s.137 regime. Stage 2 cross-pollination noted; RUN session for C12 should be aware that the broader anti-avoidance environment has tightened under FA 2026.

D-8 STAGE2_RRA2025_COMMENCEMENT_PROPAGATION — pick 1 (C9 rent-arrears) is the highest-impact RRA 2025 commencement page in the Bucket C batch. Stage 2 brief carries the 1 May 2026 commencement date + the 13-week + 3-week + UC carve-out architecture verbatim per §20.2 + §20 disambiguation table. The RUN session timing relative to commencement (today is 2026-05-27, so 3+ weeks past commencement) means the page must be written with full post-commencement framing — no 'when this comes into force' language, but 'currently in force' language. Stage 2 anti-templating watchpoint flagged.
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: C9 rent-arrears (vs landlord-tax-essentials pillar + §27 voluntary-disclosure cluster + §4 finance-cost-restriction pages); C7 investor-or-developer (vs Wave 8 Bucket B Part 8ZB / Part 9A statutory deep-dives + Wave 8 RPDT page + §5 CGT + §22.1 Pawson + §21 incorporation + §25 capital allowances); C8 short-guide-planning-permission (vs MW1 C1 CIL deep-dive + cluster change-of-use pages + C7 sibling + §18 ATED + §29 VAT change-of-use + §1 SDLT + Wave 7 capital-vs-revenue); C12 bed-and-breakfasting (vs §24 spouse cluster + §5 CGT + C7 trading-vs-investment + §22 IHT); C11 booking-com (F-102 active vs C5 airbnb-landlords + C4 air-bnb-and-post-covid + five existing serviced-accomm mechanics pages + C7 investor-or-developer); C13 commercial-service-charge (vs Wave 8 Bucket C VAT cluster + Wave 7 capital-vs-revenue + §27.7 + §25 capital allowances + C20 commonhold-and-leasehold-reform residential sibling). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; worked-example personas vary per pick (Patel monthly-rent / Ms Singh portfolio / Ms Singh airspace dev / Mr Patel BTL / Patel Holdings inserting holdco / Mr Patel + Priya daughter connected-persons / Mrs Singh mixed-use / Patel Properties Ltd commercial office / Ms Singh + Mr Patel multi-unit OTA — note: surnames recur across worked examples as anonymisation pool not unique-per-pick discipline per program convention).
